content.full_text:
FPPC

FEB 20 9:22 AM '90

THERESA D. TAYLOR
ATTORNEY AT LAW
6940 ARABELLA WAY
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA  95831
(916) 445-5957

February 16,1990


MS. KATHERINE DONOVAN
CHIEF COUNSEL
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J STREET, SUITE 800,
P.O.BOX 807
SAC. CA. 95804


DEAR MS. DONOVAN,

     I am requesting an FPPC opinion with regard to the
following questions:

     a) Under what circumstances may a private firm or
professional association, which does not have a lobbyist working in
the California Legislature, provide an honorarium to a member of
the Legislature, which might be used to pay a fine levied by a
local or state governmental entity?

     b) Under what circumstances may a private firm,
professional association, nonprofit corporation, or individual make
a monetary gift to a member of the Legislature, which might be used
to pay a fine levied by a local or state governmental entity?

     c) Under what circumstances may a private firm or
professional association participate in the establishment of an
organization or committee designed to raise money for the express
purpose of making gifts to a legislator for the purpose of paying a
fine levied by a local or state governmental entity?

     d) May a member of the Legislature use funds received as
legal honorariums or gifts to pay fines levied by a state or local
governmental entity?

     I would appreciate your earliest reply to these
inquiries. Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Theresa Taylor
Theresa Taylor

sections.question:
a) Under what circumstances may a private firm or professional association, which does not have a lobbyist working in the California Legislature, provide an honorarium to a member of the Legislature, which might be used to pay a fine levied by a local or state governmental entity?

b) Under what circumstances may a private firm, professional association, nonprofit corporation, or individual make a monetary gift to a member of the Legislature, which might be used to pay a fine levied by a local or state governmental entity?

c) Under what circumstances may a private firm or professional association participate in the establishment of an organization or committee designed to raise money for the express purpose of making gifts to a legislator for the purpose of paying a fine levied by a local or state governmental entity?

d) May a member of the Legislature use funds received as legal honorariums or gifts to pay fines levied by a state or local governmental entity?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks FPPC guidance on whether private firms, professional associations, nonprofits, and individuals may provide honorariums or gifts to California legislators that could be used to pay government-imposed fines, including whether legislators can legally use such funds for this purpose.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice is provided. The document contains four detailed questions (a-d) about honorariums, gifts, and fine payment. Document is dated February 16, 1990 and document ID is 90143.

embedding.summary: Attorney Theresa D. Taylor requests an FPPC opinion regarding the legality of providing honorariums and gifts to California legislators that could be used to pay fines, and whether legislators may legally use such funds for fine payment.
